items.title: RT by @POTUS: "I have just been informed by our Great Military that last night the Iranians shot down one of our highly sophisticated Apache Helicopters while patrolling over the Strait of Hormuz. There were two pilots involved, both are safe and uninjured. Nevertheless, the United States must, of necessity, respond to this attack." - President Donald J. Trump 🇺🇸
items.summary: "I have just been informed by our Great Military that last night the Iranians shot down one of our highly sophisticated Apache Helicopters while patrolling over the Strait of Hormuz. There were two pilots involved, both are safe and uninjured. Nevertheless, the United States must, of necessity, respond to this attack." - President Donald J. Trump 🇺🇸

items.title: Uranium mining market heats up amid nuclear - powered data centers bet
Stocks Join Bonds Higher as Oil Falls Below $90
items.summary:
A rebound on Wall Street powered ahead, with stocks joining bonds higher as oil fell amid hopes the US and Iran are getting closer to a deal to end the war that has rattled global markets.
Liz Ann Sonders, Chief Investment Strategist at Charles Schwab, discusses her midyear outlook as the market drives higher. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Bowen: Trump and Netanyahu wanted to reshape the Middle East - now they risk a permacrisis
items.summary: The US and Israeli leaders have lost control of the consequences after miscalculating the Iran war.

items.title: Vance declares U . S . will pursue Iran nuclear deal with or without Israel approval – NaturalNews . com
Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
items.summary: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

items.title: How a three - day invasion has surpassed World War One and threatens global catastrophe
Cuba Poised for Biggest US Fuel Shipment Since Cold War Embargo
items.summary: A Florida trading company is in advanced talks to send Cuba the biggest cargo of US fuel since the Eisenhower administration as the island nation contends with an acute energy crisis.

items.title: Gold Extends Fall as Renewed US-Iran Clashes Test Fragile Truce
items.summary: Gold extended a decline after the US launched fresh strikes against Iran in retaliation for the downing of a military helicopter, jeopardizing efforts to end the war that’s roiled global markets.

items.title: Malaysia Minister on Fuel Subsidy, Deficit Goals
items.summary: Malaysia may not meet its fiscal deficit targets for 2026 as the Iran war drives up the cost of fuel subsidies, Second Finance Minister Amir Hamzah Azizan exclusively tells Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin at the Invest Malaysia event. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Korea’s Kospi Extends Losses on Chipmakers, War-Tied Jitters
items.summary: South Korea’s equity benchmark slipped Wednesday, with chip stocks resuming losses and risk appetite weakening following a US strike on Iran.

items.title: China factory gate prices rise at fastest rate in 4 years
items.summary: Energy costs climb as Iran war cuts off supply through Strait of Hormuz

items.title: China Taps Commercial Oil Stockpiles to Help Weather Gulf Shock
items.summary: China has started tapping its commercial crude reserves to help offset the supply shock from the Iran war, although the world’s biggest oil importer is continuing to prioritize lower refinery use and fuel export limits to manage the fallout.

items.title: War, Volatility and a Tech Reckoning in Asia | Insight with Haslinda Amin 6/10/2026
items.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: WH Smith Cuts Outlook, Seeks Capital as Iran War Weighs on Sales
items.summary: WH Smith Plc lowered its profit outlook and set out a plan to raise capital as the fallout from the conflict in the Middle East weighs on sales at the retailer’s network of airport stores.

items.title: Singapore PM Lawrence Wong to visit Russia Kazan , first since Ukraine war sanctions
Russia - annexed Crimea War museum damaged in Ukrainian drone attack
Slowing Economy Gives Turkey Cover to Hold Interest Rates Again
items.summary: Turkey’s central bank is poised to leave its benchmark interest rate unchanged in the face of a cooling economy and subdued foreign currency demand despite rising prices driven by the Iran war.

items.title: Lee : Iran War Reduces North Korea Nuclear Deal Chances
Zelensky under pressure to end row with Poland over WW2 name of army unit
items.summary: Poland is threatening to strip Ukraine's president of a state honour over the naming Ukrainian military unit after controversial World War Two fighters.
